sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Ziperase Launches Enterprise Connector on the ServiceNow Store
feed.summary: Ziperase has launched its Enterprise Connector on the ServiceNow Store, integrating certified data erasure proof directly into ServiceNow Hardware Asset Management (HAM). This new connector eliminates the need for IT and compliance teams to manually track erasure certificates, providing automated, auditable, and accessible proof of erasure within the asset record itself. It is free for Ziperase Data Erasure Software users and compatible with ServiceNow's AI Platform Zurich release, streamlining IT asset disposition and compliance workflows.

feed.title: Ulta doubled store fulfillment capabilities in 2025
feed.summary: Ulta Beauty significantly expanded its ship-from-store program in fiscal year 2025, with over 1,000 U.S. stores now fulfilling e-commerce orders, an increase from 500 stores in 2024. This growth was aided by an AI-powered order management system, although its fulfillment center footprint remained largely the same. Other retailers like Walmart and Target are also increasingly leveraging their physical stores for fulfillment, highlighting a broader industry trend.

feed.title: Latest news: Vici acquiring Canadian portfolio; IHG adds in India, China; Marriott adds in Africa
feed.summary: Vici Properties is expanding its real estate portfolio by acquiring Canadian casino and hotel assets for $144.4 million, set to close in mid-2026. IHG Hotels & Resorts is growing its presence in India and Greater China, with new InterContinental and Atwell Suites properties. Marriott International has made its debut in Cape Verde, Africa, with a Four Points by Sheraton resort, marking its 500th select-service hotel in EMEA.

feed.title: Stratasys to support qualifcation & deployment of 3D printed parts across military platforms via US DOW contract
feed.summary: Stratasys Direct has been selected for the U.S. Department of War’s (DoW) Joint Additive Manufacturing Acceptability (JAMA) IV Pilot Parts Program. This program aims to accelerate the qualification and deployment of 3D printed parts across military platforms, expanding Stratasys's existing role in supporting defense organizations. The contract highlights increasing DoW budget allocations for additive manufacturing, with funding rising to $3.3 billion in fiscal year 2026.

feed.title: Southwest stock trails top peers as a key strategic change backfires
feed.summary: Southwest Airlines (LUV) stock has significantly underperformed its peers, dropping over 25% in the last 30 days, primarily due to rising jet fuel prices exacerbated by the ongoing Iran war. This severe impact is attributed to the company's decision last year to discontinue its fuel hedging strategy, a move that aimed to save on premiums but left it vulnerable to market volatility. While other airlines that ended hedging strategies years ago are also affected, Southwest's recent discontinuation has made it particularly susceptible, though its traditionally cheaper fares might attract more customers in the long run.

feed.title: Take-Two Stock Is Down 25% in 2026. Here’s Why GTA VI Still Dominates the Story
feed.summary: Take-Two Interactive Software (TTWO) stock has fallen 25% in 2026 despite strong fiscal third-quarter results due to investors awaiting the November release of Grand Theft Auto VI. The company's valuation depends heavily on the success of GTA VI and sustained growth in recurrent consumer spending from existing franchises. Although analysts maintain higher price targets, the stock's performance is tied to execution, release timing, and whether current games can bridge the gap until the highly anticipated launch.

feed.title: Broadcom Expands Security Reach With Symantec CBX As Shares Lag Targets
feed.summary: Broadcom has launched Symantec CBX, a new cloud-based cybersecurity platform that integrates Symantec and Carbon Black technologies, targeting mid-sized organizations with limited security resources. This move expands Broadcom's security software footprint alongside its semiconductor business. Despite recent share weakness, the company's valuation is seen as below analyst targets and an estimated fair value.

feed.title: Technical resistance and mixed momentum keep AMD stock subdued after busy product and AI news
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is currently trading at $201.22, showing a slight dip of 0.48% today. Despite launching a new CPU and receiving a boost from Meta's increased AI chip purchases, the stock faces resistance, with technical indicators suggesting sustained selling pressure and weak upside momentum. Analysts predict AMD will likely consolidate between $195.00 and $205.00 in the near term, with a downside bias prevailing.

feed.title: Video: Huntington Bank’s ‘3 Things We Learned this Week’
feed.summary: Huntington Bank's Director of Equity Research, Randy Hare, shares "The Three Things We Learned This Week" in a video. He discusses the latest manufacturing data and its implications for small businesses, provides an update on bond yields and their effect on mortgage rates for investors and prospective homebuyers, and highlights a significant breakthrough in artificial intelligence.

feed.title: The TJX Companies, Inc. Announces 13% Increase in Common Stock Dividend
feed.summary: The TJX Companies, Inc. announced a 13% increase in its quarterly common stock dividend, bringing it to $.48 per share, payable on June 4, 2026. This marks their 29th dividend increase in 30 years, demonstrating confidence in continued sales growth and profitability. Additionally, the company plans a significant share buyback program of approximately $2.50 to $2.75 billion for Fiscal 2027.

feed.title: AT&T Digital Literacy Push And Valuation Gap Draw Investor Attention
feed.summary: AT&T is collaborating with Think Together and Human-I-T to enhance digital literacy programs for K-12 students in California, a move that aligns its brand with vital social themes and could positively impact reputation. While AT&T's stock shows recent positive momentum, Simply Wall St estimates it's trading significantly below fair value, presenting a potential valuation gap. Investors are encouraged to monitor how these community investments correlate with broader business metrics, especially in light of expected earnings declines.

feed.title: Applied Automation in the Warehouse Boosts Value Across Stakeholders
feed.summary: Warehouse automation is accelerating, driven by labor shortages and the need for efficiency, and is reinforcing demand for well-located warehouse space. Contrary to concerns, automation does not reduce the need for space but rather helps solve operational constraints and expands distribution networks. Flexible automation solutions, especially AS/RS and AMR/AGV systems, are becoming more prevalent and are leading to higher retention, longer lease durations, and higher rental rates for automated facilities.

feed.title: SunocoCorp (NYSE:SUNC) Reaches New 1-Year High - Here's Why
feed.summary: SunocoCorp (NYSE:SUNC) reached a new 52-week high of $63.65 during Monday's trading, closing at $62.8930 on significant volume. The stock currently holds a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with a $64.00 price target, though analysts have mixed views, including a recent upgrade to "strong-buy" from Raymond James and a downgrade to "strong sell" from Zacks. Major institutional investors like Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley initiated substantial positions in SUNC during the fourth quarter.

feed.title: American Water Marks 140 Years of Safe, Reliable Water and Wastewater Service to Customers and Communities in 2026 – Company Announcement - FT.com
feed.summary: American Water is commemorating 140 years of providing safe, clean, reliable, and affordable water and wastewater services. The company plans to invest up to $48 billion in infrastructure upgrades and regulated acquisitions over the next decade. American Water also recognized the 15th anniversary of its Charitable Foundation and the 45th anniversary of its Research & Development Program.

feed.title: Uber Blacklane Deal Puts Focus On Luxury Growth And Valuation Gap
feed.summary: Uber Technologies is acquiring Blacklane, a premium chauffeur service, to expand its presence in the luxury and executive travel market. This move represents a strategic shift for Uber, aiming for higher-margin customers beyond its core ride-hailing business. The deal could impact Uber's revenue mix and offers a potential valuation upside, as the stock currently trades significantly below analyst targets and estimated fair value despite recent short-term declines.

feed.title: NYSE Content Update: Koppers to Ring Closing Bell for 20th Listing Anniversary
feed.summary: The New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) released its daily pre-market update, noting rising markets despite ongoing Middle East conflict. Koppers (NYSE: KOP) CEO Leroy Ball will ring the closing bell to celebrate the company's 20th listing anniversary. The update also highlighted discussions on AI's business impact and sustainable farming practices.

feed.title: Jack Henry & Associates: Quality Remains, But Growth Is Slowing (NASDAQ:JKHY)
feed.summary: Jack Henry & Associates (JKHY) demonstrates stable operational momentum with strong Q2 revenues and EPS, yet it faces structural growth headwinds. The company's business model is characterized by 90% recurring revenues and high margins, but its single-digit revenue growth and modest EPS growth limit its upside. Despite a current valuation of 24x P/E, which is a discount to historical multiples, the valuation is still considered elevated for a defensive, low-growth profile, prompting a neutral stance on the stock.

feed.title: How Amex exploits new AI tools
feed.summary: American Express is extensively integrating artificial intelligence across its operations, identifying "hundreds" of use cases in areas like sales, engineering, and customer service. CEO Steve Squeri highlighted that AI tools help Amex travel advisors provide faster recommendations, engineers trim coding times by over 30%, and sales teams generate leads and automate follow-ups. Despite widespread concerns about AI-driven job losses, Amex emphasizes that its AI adoption focuses on enhancing tools and deepening customer relationships rather than significantly reducing headcount.

feed.title: Verizon to report earnings April 27, 2026
feed.summary: Verizon Communications Inc. (VZ) announced it will report its first-quarter 2026 earnings on Monday, April 27, 2026. The company will host a webcast at 8:30 a.m. ET to present results, with materials available on its Investor Relations website an hour and a half prior. Verizon generated revenues of $138.2 billion in 2025 by providing mobile and network connectivity services to millions of customers worldwide.

feed.title: American Water Celebrates 140 Years of Service
feed.summary: American Water is celebrating its 140th anniversary by outlining significant investments, customer growth, and its ongoing commitment to innovation and community development. The company plans to invest up to $48 billion in infrastructure upgrades and acquisitions over the next decade, while also highlighting the addition of approximately 385,000 new customers through acquisitions over the past ten years. Analyst ratings for AWK are mixed, with current forecasts suggesting a potential stock price fall among moderate sell recommendations.

feed.title: Dow Rallies 375 Points On Gains For Walt Disney, Travelers Shares
feed.summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average rallied 375 points, driven by gains in shares of Walt Disney and Travelers. This news article also briefly covers Unity Software's fourth-quarter financial results, where the company reported a 35% year-over-year revenue increase to $609 million, surpassing analyst estimates, despite a quarterly loss of 66 cents per share.

feed.title: Verizon to post quarterly results April 27 before 8:30 a.m. webcast
feed.summary: Verizon Communications Inc. (NYSE, Nasdaq: VZ) announced it will report first-quarter 2026 earnings on Monday, April 27, 2026. A webcast of the results will begin at 8:30 a.m. ET, with accompanying materials available on Verizon's Investor Relations website starting at 7:00 a.m. ET. The company also disclosed that it generated $138.2 billion in revenue for 2025.

feed.title: Chipotle brings back digital ‘Burrito Vault’ gamified loyalty promotion
feed.summary: Chipotle is bringing back its "Burrito Vault" gamified loyalty promotion for the third consecutive year to celebrate National Burrito Day. The promotion, running through April 1, allows Chipotle Rewards members to win prizes by guessing burrito order combinations, with a new "Double Protein Power Hour" this year. The company will also offer a $0 delivery fee on digital orders on National Burrito Day, April 2.

feed.title: Fastenal Company Announces Conference Call to Review 2026 First Quarter Earnings
feed.summary: Fastenal Company (Nasdaq:FAST) has announced a conference call to discuss its 2026 first-quarter financial results and current operations. The live webcast is scheduled for Monday, April 13, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. central time, with an accompanying presentation available at 6:00 a.m. central time. An online archive of the call will be accessible shortly after its conclusion until June 1, 2026.

feed.title: Important Notice to Long-Term Shareholders of BellRing
feed.summary: Grabar Law Office is investigating potential breaches of fiduciary duty by officers and directors of BellRing Brands, Inc. (NYSE: BRBR), Coty Inc. (NYSE: COTY), Gartner, Inc. (NYSE: IT), and Ramaco Resources, Inc. (NASDAQ: METC). Shareholders who purchased shares before specific dates and still hold them may be eligible to seek corporate reforms and other remedies. The investigations stem from alleged materially false or misleading statements and failures to disclose adverse facts about the companies' business, operations, and prospects.

feed.title: Form 424B2 CITIGROUP INC
feed.summary: Citigroup Global Markets Holdings Inc., guaranteed by Citigroup Inc., is offering Buffered Autocallable Securities due May 5, 2031, linked to the S&P 500 Futures 40% Intraday Edge Volatility TCA 6% Decrement Index (USD) ER. These debt securities do not pay interest, may be automatically redeemed early at a premium, and expose investors to potential principal loss if the underlying index depreciates beyond a 20% buffer. The underlying index is highly risky due to leveraged exposure to the S&P 500 Futures Excess Return Index, subject to notional costs and a 6% annual decrement, which can significantly underperform the S&P 500 Index.

feed.title: Intuit's AI Transformation: Will It Drive Future Growth?
feed.summary: Intuit is heavily investing in AI to enhance its financial software products like TurboTax and QuickBooks, expecting this transformation to drive future growth. The company has partnered with Anthropic to integrate Claude's AI capabilities for mid-market clients, launched an AI-native ERP solution for the construction industry, and is seeing strong performance across its core segments due to AI integration. Despite a recent slight decline in share price, Intuit is trading at a discount to the industry average and analysts project continued double-digit growth.

feed.title: Microsoft Stock Decline Amid Cloud Competition & AI Risks | 2026 Analysis - News and Statistics
feed.summary: Microsoft (MSFT) shares recently dropped seven percent in a week and over 25% year-to-date despite strong Q2 financial results, including a 17% revenue increase and 26% growth in Microsoft Cloud revenue. The decline is attributed to intensifying cloud competition, with Google Cloud's revenue growth accelerating faster than Azure, and long-term risks posed by AI's rapid evolution to Microsoft's software-as-a-service business model.

feed.title: Earnings Preview: What To Expect From Rollins' Report
feed.summary: Rollins, Inc. (ROL), a global pest and wildlife control service provider with a $25.3 billion market cap, is projected to announce Q1 2026 adjusted EPS of $0.24, an increase of 9.1% year-over-year. Despite lagging behind the S&P 500 and the Industrial Select Sector SPDR ETF over the past year, analysts hold a "Moderate Buy" rating for ROL stock, expecting a potential upside of 23.4% from current levels. The company's Q4 2025 results saw a decline in shares due to weaker-than-expected revenue and profitability, primarily attributed to erratic weather affecting higher-margin work.

feed.title: 3 Companies Built Their Fortunes on COVID Vaccines, but Only 1 Has a Real Plan for What Comes Next
feed.summary: This article examines three companies that initially made fortunes from COVID-19 vaccines: Moderna (MRNA), Novavax (NVAX), and BioNTech (BNTX). While all faced challenges post-pandemic, the author argues only BioNTech has a robust plan for future growth, primarily through a strong oncology pipeline backed by a substantial balance sheet. Moderna shows recent momentum but struggles with cash burn, while Novavax is profitable but faces high volatility and dependence on partnerships.

feed.title: Pharma Stocks Pfizer, GSK Offer Defensive Plays in an Uncertain Market: Charts
feed.summary: Pharmaceutical stocks like Pfizer and GSK are gaining renewed investor interest as potential defensive plays in an uncertain market. Their steady demand, resilient earnings, and lower sensitivity to economic cycles make them attractive amid increasing market volatility and unclear growth expectations.

feed.title: Declining Equity and Credit Markets in 2026 Will Affect T. Rowe Price's Flows and AUM Levels
feed.summary: Morningstar believes that while T. Rowe Price faces challenges from declining equity and credit markets in 2026, it is well-positioned among US-based traditional asset managers. The company's strengths include its operational scale, strong brands, ability to deliver above-average active fund outperformance, reasonable fees, and a sticky client base primarily from retirement accounts. Despite potential market headwinds, T. Rowe Price's fundamental business strategy is expected to help mitigate the impact on its flows and assets under management.

feed.title: MORNSTAR: Blackstone's Private Credit Exposure Creates Headwinds for Fundraising and Will Increase Redemptions
feed.summary: Blackstone, a preeminent alternative-asset manager with $1.275 trillion in assets under management, faces headwinds due to its significant private credit exposure. This exposure is expected to create challenges for fundraising efforts and lead to increased redemptions. The company's fair value estimate has been lowered by 17% as a result of these private credit concerns.

feed.title: [6-K] SASOL LTD Current Report (Foreign Issuer) | SSL SEC Filing - Form 6-K
feed.summary: This article reports on a 6-K SEC filing by SASOL LTD (SSL), a foreign issuer. The filing was made on March 30, 2026, at 10:40 AM, and is categorized as neutral in both impact and sentiment. The article also provides an overview of SSL's stock performance, recent news, and other SEC filings.

feed.title: Wheaton Precious Metals vs. SSR Mining: Which Mining Stock Wins Now?
feed.summary: This article analyzes Wheaton Precious Metals (WPM) and SSR Mining (SSRM), two prominent gold and silver mining companies, against the backdrop of surging precious metal prices. It compares their fundamentals, growth drivers, and potential challenges, concluding that while both are set to benefit, SSRM's stronger price performance and more attractive valuation give it an edge despite both holding a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold). The article details each company's production outlook, financial performance, and strategic initiatives, including SSRM's acquisition of Cripple Creek & Victor mine and WPM's diversified asset portfolio.

feed.title: Sensei Biotherapeutics Inc reports results for the quarter ended December 31 - Earnings Summary
feed.summary: Sensei Biotherapeutics Inc (SNSE) has released its earnings summary for the quarter that ended on December 31. The report was provided by Refinitiv and is a concise overview of the company's financial performance.

feed.title: Papa Johns Turns Up the Heat on Menu Innovation with Bold New Launch: Oven‑Toasted Sandwiches
feed.summary: Papa Johns has debuted new Oven-Toasted Sandwiches, marking an expansion beyond pizza into a new menu category. The company aims to redefine the sandwich market with higher-quality ingredients and an indulgent experience. This launch signifies a further evolution for the brand, emphasizing its "better ingredients" promise.

feed.title: QQQI ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns BOATS:QQQI
feed.summary: The NEOS Nasdaq 100 High Income ETF (QQQI) is an actively managed ETF that invests in Nasdaq-100 Index stocks, using NDX call options to generate high monthly income. It focuses on tax efficiency through Section 1256 Contracts and aims for tax-optimized tracking of the index. As of March 26, 2026, QQQI has significant exposure to Electronic Technology and Technology Services, with top holdings including NVIDIA Corporation and Apple Inc., and offers a dividend yield of 15.42%.

feed.title: Quick ratio of Zoetis, Inc. Class A – BSESOF:ZOE
feed.summary: This article provides the quick ratio for Zoetis, Inc. Class A (BSESOF:ZOE). It focuses on financial data for the company, accessible through TradingView. The page emphasizes market data and financial metrics for investors.

feed.title: Coca-Cola Stock Is Up 10% in 2026. Here’s Why Analysts See a $90 Target
feed.summary: Coca-Cola's stock has risen 10% in 2026, with analysts setting a $90 target, implying a 19.2% upside over 2.8 years. This is driven by defensive investor demand, strategic investments in high-growth brands like Fairlife, and marketing initiatives like the FIFA World Cup. Despite strong operational performance and profitability, the stock's current valuation, trading at a premium, leaves less room for error, with future growth dependent on sustained earnings and managing cost pressures.

feed.title: Analog Devices' Industrial Segment Picks Up Pace: What Lies Ahead?
feed.summary: Analog Devices' (ADI) industrial segment experienced substantial growth, increasing by 38% year over year and becoming its largest revenue driver. This growth is fueled by demand across various sectors including instrumentation, automation, healthcare, energy management, and defense modernization. The company's robust performance, favorable valuation, and positive earnings estimates suggest continued strong prospects for ADI.

feed.title: Exxon Mobil, Pepsico among market cap stock movers on Monday
feed.summary: This article highlights major stock movers on Monday, March 30, 2026, categorizing them by market capitalization. Exxon Mobil (XOM) and Pepsico (PEP) are noted among the mega-cap stocks that are rallying, while others like Micron Tech (MU) are falling. The report also details movements in large-cap, mid-cap, and small-cap stocks, including specific company news and analyst rating changes impacting their performance.

feed.title: Beat the Market the Zacks Way: Equinix, Archrock, Cheniere Energy in Focus
feed.summary: This article highlights how Zacks Investment Research's methodologies, including Zacks Rank, Zacks Recommendation, Focus List, ECAP, and ECDP, have successfully guided investors through recent market volatility. It cites specific stock examples like Equinix, Archrock, and Cheniere Energy that have seen significant gains after being recommended by Zacks, outperforming the S&P 500. The article also touches upon current market conditions characterized by uncertainty, rising oil prices, and concerns about stagflation.

feed.title: AXS Stock Trading at a Discount to Industry at 1.31X: Time to Buy?
feed.summary: AXS Capital Holdings Limited (AXS) shares are trading at a discount compared to the Zacks Property and Casualty Insurance industry, with a forward price-to-book value of 1.31X and a Value Score of B. The company shows encouraging growth projections for 2026 and 2027, optimistic analyst sentiment with a potential 25% upside, and a higher return on capital, alongside an impressive 18-year dividend growth history. These factors, combined with its focus on strategic growth areas and efficiency, suggest AXS is a compelling investment opportunity.

feed.title: Why Are Micron Technology Shares Sliding On Monday?
feed.summary: Micron Technology (NASDAQ: MU) shares fell after Google unveiled TurboQuant, an AI memory compression algorithm, which analysts believe could reduce the demand for memory chips. While some analysts are concerned about "demand destruction," others suggest efficiency gains could lead to increased consumption. The article also notes that Monday is the ex-dividend date for Micron's quarterly payout.

feed.title: Wabtec Announces First Quarter 2026 Earnings Release Date
feed.summary: Wabtec Corporation will release its first quarter 2026 financial results on Friday, April 25, 2026, followed by a conference call with investors and analysts. This announcement is significant for those monitoring the transportation industry, as Wabtec's performance is often seen as an indicator of the sector's health and future trends. Investors and analysts will closely watch the report for insights into the company's outlook and the broader transportation technology sector.

feed.title: Corteva panelists to present Hertz Lecture at Iowa State April 14
feed.summary: Three senior leaders from Corteva Agriscience — Geoff Graham, Jeremy Dirks, and Mark Deterding — will participate in the 2026 Carl and Marjory Hertz Lecture on Emerging Issues in Agriculture at Iowa State University on April 14. They will discuss Pioneer's historical role in the seed industry, its partnership with Iowa State, and its evolution to meet farmer needs. The event is free, open to the public, and offers scholarship opportunities for attending students.

feed.title: PepsiCo Could Climb 10% to $169 as Deutsche Bank Rates the Stock a Buy
feed.summary: Deutsche Bank maintains a Buy rating for PepsiCo (PEP) with a $169 price target, representing a 10% upside potential despite recent stock pullbacks. The company's consistent dividend increases, international growth, and strategic investments are key drivers. Reaching this target hinges on organic revenue growth, EPS growth, and stabilization of North American volumes, though consumer trade-down and commodity headwinds pose risks.

feed.title: Jack in the Box names CMO to strengthen product innovation, loyalty
feed.summary: Jack in the Box has appointed Katelyn Zborowski as its new Chief Marketing Officer, aiming to enhance product innovation and customer loyalty. Zborowski, who previously spent over a decade at Yum Brands (Taco Bell, Pizza Hut), is credited with developing numerous limited-time offerings that boosted sales. Her appointment comes as Jack in the Box navigates a challenging quick-service restaurant environment marked by price-sensitive consumers and increased competition.

feed.title: Spotlight on Central Florida: A conversation with Scott Cathcart, Florida Regional President at Truist
feed.summary: Scott Cathcart, Florida Regional President at Truist, discusses the strong business momentum in Central Florida, driven by population growth and sector diversification. He highlights key opportunities like access to capital for acquisitions and challenges such as talent availability and high insurance costs. Cathcart also details how Truist is partnering with businesses in the region, focusing on relationship-driven advisory models and supporting local economic development.
